author: TNN
title: Udhayanidhi Confident DMK Front Will Win 200 Seats in 2026 Assembly Elections | Trichy News - Times of India
text: Trichy: Deputy chief minister Udhayanidhi Stalin has expressed confidence that the DMK alliance would secure 200 seats in the next assembly elections and retain power. \"Like our victory in all 40 constituencies in the Lok Sabha elections, let us commence preparations forthwith and triumph in more than 200 constituencies in the 2026 Assembly elections,\" he said at a public gathering following the inauguration of former chief minister M Karunanidhi's statue at Thuraiyur bus stand in Trichy. This was Udhayanidhi's first visit to Thuraiyur after minister KN Nehru's son K N Arun Nehru won the Perambalur Lok Sabha seat under which the area falls. In his brief address, Udhayanidhi hit out at AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami for criticising DMK govt regarding naming of govt schemes after Karunanidhi. \"I acknowledge that I lack his credentials or attributes as Leader of the Opposition. Unlike him, I never prostrated at anyone's feet to acquire this position. While holding various responsibilities, being Kalaignar's grandson and a member of this family remains my utmost pride,\" stated Udhayanidhi. Earlier, Udhayanidhi was accorded a reception by party cadres at Trichy international airport before he proceeded to inaugurate Kalaignar library on hospital road in Thuraiyur on behalf of DMK youth wing. He also opened the MP office of Arun Nehru before unveiling Karunanidhi's statue.Senior ministers K N Nehru, S Regupathy, S S Sivasankar, Anbil Mahesh Poyyamozhi, Siva V Meyyanathan and Govi Chezhian attended the programme.
